difficultyAudit.phase2Policy: hard or expert only — medium is prohibited
difficultyAudit.result: PASS — all 178 questions are hard or expert. No medium questions generated.

duplicationReport.methodology: Each question was assigned a unique stemAnchor combining chief complaint + patient demographic + key clinical finding or decision point. Cross-reference was maintained against Phase 1 stem anchors from np-phase1-questions-part1.json and np-phase1-questions-part2.json.

notableNearDuplicateResolvedPairs.resolution: np-p2-001 (emergency with papilledema) vs np-p2-009 (urgency without papilledema) — differentiated by end-organ damage presence/absence; stems not duplicated
Phase 1 np-p1-001 (headache differential SAH vs GCA vs thunderclap) vs Phase 2 np-p2-011 (TIA disposition ABCD2) — entirely different clinical scenarios; no duplication

Phase 1 mdd-diagnosis questions tested PHQ-9 scoring and MDD diagnosis. Phase 2 np-p2-049 to np-p2-055 test C-SSRS stratification and involuntary hold triggers — no content overlap. SF-04 boundary maintained.

Phase 2 Part A T2DM questions (np-p2-105 to np-p2-118) stay within SF-01 slugA scope — map-level class selection, A1c targets, SU renal safety, metformin thresholds. No cardiorenal trial data, no GLP-1/SGLT-2 mechanism or AE profiles.

np-p2-119 to np-p2-124 (2A) = TSH interpretation, treatment initiation decision, Graves vs Hashimoto. np-p2-125 to np-p2-130 (2B) = absorption interactions, dose adjustment, monitoring interval. SF-02 slugA/slugB boundary cleanly maintained.

auditNotes.detail: T2DM Phase 2 questions (np-p2-105 to np-p2-118) contain map-level SGLT-2 and GLP-1 references only (e.g., 'SGLT-2 when established ASCVD/HF/CKD present') without mechanism, AE profile, or cardiorenal trial data. Specific trial data (EMPA-REG, LEADER, etc.) explicitly excluded.

2A questions (np-p2-119 to np-p2-124) test TSH interpretation, treatment initiation decision, Graves recognition, nodule workup. 2B questions (np-p2-125 to np-p2-130) test levothyroxine dosing, absorption interactions, monitoring intervals, pregnancy adjustment. No cross-contamination found.

CKD Phase 2 questions test KDIGO staging, renoprotective agents (RAAS, SGLT-2), dietary management, anemia, MBD, and metabolic acidosis. Cockcroft-Gault formula calculations, antibiotic dose adjustments, and DOAC dose tables explicitly excluded — belong to SF-03 slugB which is reserved for a future Phase 3 lesson.

Psychiatric Red Flags 2A (np-p2-049 to np-p2-055) tests C-SSRS use, involuntary hold criteria, Tarasoff duty, and prescribing safety (TCA supply limit). Antidepressant mechanism, SSRI/SNRI selection, or augmentation strategies were NOT included — those belong to antidepressant-selection topic (SF-04 slugB, planned for Phase 3).

HTN Red Flags questions (np-p2-001 to np-p2-010) test urgency vs emergency distinction, end-organ damage recognition, secondary HTN screen, white coat HTN. HTN Step Therapy questions (np-p2-077 to np-p2-090) test first-line drug selection, compelling indications, step therapy, resistant HTN, special populations. Clean boundary maintained.

2A questions (np-p2-063 to np-p2-069) test EF-based classification, BNP interpretation, NYHA staging, decompensation triggers, and phenotype-based clinical decisions. 2B questions (np-p2-070 to np-p2-076) test four-pillar GDMT, ARNi washout requirement, evidence-based BB selection, MRA contraindications, SGLT-2 indication without DM. Clean separation maintained at the lesson-part boundary.

shortfalls.note: Question np-p2-094 contains a complex answer rationale note about TEE-guided vs 3-week strategy — both B and D are guideline-acceptable approaches; the question is flagged for potential editorial review to ensure answer key clarity.
COPD topic is at saturation for primary care NP scope. Phase 3 would need different anchors (pulmonary rehabilitation details, NIV settings, palliative specifics) to avoid duplication.

saturationPoints.note: 14 questions fully cover phenotype classification, four GDMT pillars, ARNi washout, evidence-based BB, MRA contraindications, SGLT-2 for HF. Additional questions in Phase 3 would need to focus on device therapy indications (CRT, ICD) or advanced heart failure (inotrope support, transplant evaluation) to avoid re-testing same prescribing concepts.

14 questions cover first-line by race/comorbidity, ACEi cough switch, pregnancy contraindication, CCB edema, step therapy, resistant HTN, NSAID-induced HTN, CKD/DM special populations, post-stroke secondary prevention, thiazide-gout management. At high saturation for primary care ambulatory scope.

12 questions cover IDA (alternate-day dosing), B12 deficiency with neuropathy, mixed anemia differential, G6PD, TTP, warm AIHA, sickle cell ACS, ACD vs IDA, pernicious anemia post-gastrectomy, sideroblastic/lead poisoning, phenytoin-folate, normocytic mixed anemia. At full saturation for primary care scope. Phase 3 would need to expand into bone marrow biopsy-level differentiation (beyond NP primary care scope) or polycythemia vera.

12 questions cover TSH interpretation, subclinical hypothyroidism thresholds, Graves vs Hashimoto, nodule FNAB indications, central hypothyroidism, overtreatment AF risk, depression-thyroid intersection, calcium absorption interaction, cardiac dosing, pregnancy increase, rifampin CYP induction, PPI absorption, brand-to-generic switch. At saturation for outpatient NP scope.

topicsNotCoveredInPhase2.reason: SF-01 slugB — GLP-1/SGLT-2 mechanism, cardiorenal trial data (EMPA-REG, LEADER, CREDENCE, SUSTAIN, REWIND), and comorbidity-specific selection are scoped to this separate lesson. Planned for Phase 3 Prescribing & Advanced Pharmacology domain.
SF-03 slugB — Cockcroft-Gault calculations, antibiotic dose adjustments by eGFR, DOAC dose tables, opioid adjustment. Planned for Phase 3.
SF-04 slugB — SSRI/SNRI selection, titration, augmentation, discontinuation syndrome. Planned for Phase 3 Mental Health Prescribing domain.

SF-06 cross-topic fence — drug-specific monitoring, pharmacokinetics, and AE management for ACEi/ARB/ARNi at the agent level. Phase 2 HF questions test the DECISION of which class to add; Phase 3 Prescribing would test AE management per individual drug.

Identified in Phase 1 readiness report as Phase 2 Prescribing domain. Covered in Phase 1 readiness report's phase2ExecutionOrder. Planned for Phase 3 given the Prescribing & Advanced Pharmacology domain is not the primary focus of Phase 2 as defined by user prompt.

Partially covered in afib-rate-rhythm-stroke-risk-2A (anticoagulation decision, DOAC selection, reversal agents, cardioversion protocol). Full prescribing-level detail (dose adjustment by renal function, drug interactions by DOAC class, perioperative management) reserved for Phase 3 Prescribing domain.
Identified in Phase 1 execution order. Was not part of the user-defined Phase 2 scope (Red Flags & Safety + Core CDM). Planned as a dedicated Phase 3 Safety topic.

SF05.boundary: HTN staging/urgency recognition vs drug class selection and step therapy

SF06.boundary: HF phenotype classification/BNP/NYHA vs four-pillar GDMT drug decisions

conditions.check: Phase 1 (133 questions, np-p1-001 to np-p1-133) + Phase 2 (178 questions, np-p2-001 to np-p2-178) = 311 total NP-level questions with no ID gaps or duplicates

conditions.description: All 6 scope fences active and validated
conditions.check: SF-01 through SF-06 all represented in np-scope-fences.json; Phase 2 questions were generated with all fences enforced; zero violations detected

Phase 1 lessons/questions test RECOGNITION and DIFFERENTIAL REASONING. Phase 2 2A RF&S questions test recognition + disposition actions. Phase 2 CDM 2A/2B lessons maintain phenotype recognition vs pharmacotherapy separation per SF-06 template. Management prescribing questions live exclusively in Phase 2 CDM.
All 178 Phase 2 questions are hard or expert. Zero medium difficulty questions were generated.
All Phase 2 question topicSlug values match corresponding lesson outlines in np-phase2-lesson-outlines.json. No new slugs introduced that are not in the blueprint.

conditions.status: OUTSTANDING — low priority; can trail Phase 3 launch
conditions.action: Verify rash-differential-primary-care slug consistency across np-phase1-lesson-outlines.json, np-phase1-questions-part1.json, and blueprint

PrescribingAndAdvancedPharmacology.rationale: SF-01 slugB — most clinically impactful prescribing lesson; GLP-1 RAs (semaglutide, liraglutide, dulaglutide) and SGLT-2 inhibitors (empagliflozin, dapagliflozin, canagliflozin) with cardiorenal trial integration by comorbidity
CAP, SSTI, UTI, sinusitis, pharyngitis antibiotic selection with resistance stewardship principles; CURB-65, Centor/McIsaac guideline application
Warfarin, insulin, digoxin, lithium, phenytoin — ISMP high-alert medication prescribing safety; narrow therapeutic index monitoring; drug-drug interactions
SF-03 slugB — Cockcroft-Gault calculations; antibiotic dose adjustment by eGFR; DOAC dose tables in CKD; opioid adjustment; metformin rules
SF-04 slugB — SSRI/SNRI selection by patient profile (side effect profile, drug interactions, special populations); augmentation strategies; discontinuation syndrome
Expand beyond AF-specific DOAC content from Phase 2 — VTE prophylaxis, PE/DVT treatment, drug interactions per DOAC (P-gp, CYP3A4), perioperative management tables

AcutePrimaryCareAndUrgentCare.rationale: CAP CURB-65 risk stratification, antibiotic selection by severity and outpatient vs inpatient, atypical coverage
UTI antibiotic selection, complicated vs uncomplicated, catheter-associated, recurrent UTI prevention, Foley management

MentalHealthPrescribing.rationale: Phase 3 dedicated antidepressant prescribing lesson — SSRI vs SNRI vs atypicals by comorbidity and patient profile

phase3ReadinessVerdict.summaryStatement: Phase 2 is complete with 178 questions across 16 topic-lesson-parts in two domains (Red Flags & Safety: 62 questions; Core Chronic Disease Management: 116 questions). All 6 scope fences (SF-01 through SF-06) are active and validated with zero violations. All Phase 2 questions are hard or expert difficulty. No duplicate stems were detected against Phase 1 inventory. The recognition-vs-management separation established in Phase 1 is maintained and extended by the SF-06 lesson-part-level boundary within heart-failure-gdmt-primary-care. Outstanding pre-Phase-3 items: (1) 14 Phase 1 shortfall questions (ADJ-01) remain ungenerated — these should be completed before Phase 3 launch; (2) Slug consistency check (ADJ-02) is low priority and can trail Phase 3. Phase 3 is recommended to begin with the Prescribing & Advanced Pharmacology domain, prioritizing GLP-1/SGLT-2 comorbidity selection (SF-01 slugB), antibiotic stewardship, high-alert medications, and medication dose adjustment in CKD (SF-03 slugB).
